name: GPT-5.5 (xHigh)
archDisplay: Undisclosed architecture (presumed dense transformer, reasoning-tuned); codename "Spud"; 'xhigh' (maximum) reasoning-effort configuration
note: Highest reasoning-effort sibling of the base 'GPT-5.5' entry ('reserved for the hardest quality-first workloads' per OpenAI's own guidance). Same weights/pricing/context as the default and High siblings; distinguished by reasoning.effort=xhigh.

results.notes: Explicitly attributed to 'xhigh effort' by benchlm.ai. Numerically identical to the frontiermath-tier1-3 result already recorded on the base 'GPT-5.5' corpus entry -- see verificationNotes.
Explicitly attributed to 'xhigh effort' by benchlm.ai. Numerically identical to the frontiermath-tier4 result already recorded on the base 'GPT-5.5' corpus entry -- see verificationNotes.

Agents' Last Exam (ALE-CLI, Overall leaderboard), Codex harness, XHigh reasoning effort: Pass rate 26.6% / Score 47.9% (rank 10). Matches this catalog entry's own effortLabel ('xHigh'). Score component used per corpus convention.

GDPval-AA v2 (Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index v4.1 component), independently measured on the dedicated 'GPT-5.5 (xhigh)' AA model page (URL slug and page title both confirm this is the xhigh-effort config, distinct from the 'GPT-5.5 (high)' page which scores differently on the same eval). Read by exact rank-position matching against AA's live ranked benchmark chart (GPT-5.5 (xhigh) placed between Claude Opus 4.7 (max) and GPT-5.5 (high), both at 50%). Not previously tracked on this catalog entry. In the GDPval-AA v2 ranked bar chart (29 models), 'GPT-5.5 (xhigh)' sits at rank 16 with a score of 50% (an integer, tied with Claude Opus 4.7 (max) directly above it at 50%; GPT-5.5 (high)]

Terminal-Bench v2.1 (AA's harness), independently measured on the 'GPT-5.5 (xhigh)' AA model page. Distinct data point from OpenAI's own self-reported 'Terminal-Bench 2.0: 82.7%' figure (different harness version/measurement); this catalog entry currently has no terminal-bench-2 result at all, so this is new, not a duplicate.

Humanity's Last Exam, independently measured on the 'GPT-5.5 (xhigh)' AA model page (AA's own methodology, likely differs from OpenAI's no-tools condition -- OpenAI's own blog cites 41.4%/52.2% depending on source, none of which is on this catalog entry). Not previously tracked here.

GPQA Diamond, independently measured on the 'GPT-5.5 (xhigh)' AA model page -- ranked #1-2 of 28 models tracked (94%, tied with GPT-5.6 Sol (max)). Not previously tracked on this catalog entry. The underlying chart JSON confirms GPQA Diamond=0.935353535353535 for label 'GPT-5.5 (xhigh)' (detailsUrl /models/gpt-5]

verificationNotes:
Confirmed as a distinct, independently-benchmarked configuration on Artificial Analysis (model page 'GPT-5.5 (xhigh)', explicitly labeled 'Reasoning Effort Label: xhigh' on direct fetch; Intelligence Index score 55, rank #7/188, strictly ahead of the 'high' sibling's 53/#12). benchlm.ai's benchmark table for GPT-5.5 explicitly attributes its FrontierMath v2 figures to 'xhigh effort' (Tier 1-3: 51.7%, Tier 4: 35.4%) — these are numerically identical to the FrontierMath scores already stored on the pre-existing base 'GPT-5.5' corpus entry, strongly suggesting that entry's headline benchmark numbers are actually xhigh-tier results mislabeled as the plain/default config. Flagging for the ingesting pipeline to consider migrating those two FrontierMath results (and possibly GDPval/BrowseComp/SWE-Bench Pro/Terminal-Bench, which are likely also xhigh headline figures) from the base entry onto this xHigh entry, and to set the base entry's isBestConfig to false since this sibling is now the true best-of-cohort.

2026-07-16 core-benchmark completeness check (mmlu, math, aime, humaneval, livecodebench, swe-bench, mmmu): all seven resolve to omitted_no_evidence -- no citable xhigh-specific (or even effort-unspecified) score exists for any of them. (1) Official materials: three independent close-readings of openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-5-5 (kingy.ai's '9 numbers quoted verbatim from OpenAI's evaluation tables', vellum.ai's 16-benchmark comprehensive listing, and alexlavaee.me's review) all enumerate OpenAI's launch benchmarks in full and none lists MMLU, MATH/MATH-500, AIME, HumanEval, LiveCodeBench, SWE-bench Verified, or MMMU -- OpenAI's own launch page evidently never reported these seven. (2) Independent leaderboards: Artificial Analysis maintains dedicated AIME-2025, LiveCodeBench, and MATH-500 leaderboards but each is capped at a static 7-of-269/343/201 'spotlight' model set that excludes every GPT-5.5 variant entirely -- AA has not run these three evals on GPT-5.5 at all. AA's own 'GPT-5.5 (xhigh)' model page (fetched live) lists its full tracked-eval set (GDPval-AA v2, tau3-Banking, Terminal-Bench v2.1, SciCode, HLE, GPQA Diamond, CritPt, AA-Omniscience, AA-LCR, AA-Briefcase, MMMU-Pro) and it contains no MMLU, base MMMU, HumanEval, or SWE-bench (classic/Verified) entry -- AA has fully retired these in favor of harder successors (MMLU-Pro, MMMU-Pro, SWE-bench Pro, etc.) for frontier-tier models by mid-2026. (3) Sibling cross-reference: neither the base 'GPT-5.5' nor 'GPT-5.5 (High)' corpus entries carry any of these seven benchmarks either, a consistent absence across the whole family already vetted in prior rounds. One contested exception was investigated and rejected: TokenMix.ai and marc0.dev both circulate an unsourced 'SWE-Bench Verified 88.7% / MMLU 92.4%' pair attributed loosely to 'OpenAI announcement', but this pair appears nowhere in the three careful launch-post transcriptions above, is not effort-specific, and could not be corroborated on OpenAI's own page (blocked from direct fetch) or on Vals.ai/epoch.ai (access blocked/no data returned) -- treated as unreliable and not added. This matches the taxonomy's own framing of mmlu ('largely saturated at the frontier') and humaneval ('long-saturated at the frontier') as benchmarks frontier labs are expected to stop publishing.

2026-07-17 second-pass deep dive on mmlu/math/aime/humaneval, using source types NOT touched by the 2026-07-16 pass (official launch page, Epoch AI, Vals.ai, LMArena, BenchLM). All four gaps still resolve to omitted_no_evidence after checking all 6 prescribed new source types:

(1) Stanford HELM: Direct fetch of crfm.stanford.edu/helm/lite/latest/groups/core_scenarios.json returned HTTP 404 (path/schema has moved or requires JS rendering); web search against crfm.stanford.edu confirms HELM's evaluated-model rosters (Capabilities, Lite) list GPT-4o/Claude/Gemini-era models but no GPT-5.5 of any effort variant -- HELM has not run this model at all, so no independent MMLU (or any) score exists there to retrieve.

(2) arXiv / Semantic Scholar: Targeted arXiv searches ("GPT-5.5 MMLU", AIME/MATH/HumanEval combinations) surface only GPT-5/GPT-5.1/GPT-5.2 papers -- no arXiv paper was found reporting a GPT-5.5 baseline number on any of the four benchmarks. The one paper that does cite "GPT-5.5" in its bibliography (arXiv 2605.00674, "Beyond Benchmarks: MathArena...") was fetched directly and confirmed to have no GPT-5.5 row in its actual results tables, only a bibliographic citation. (semanticscholar.org direct fetch was blocked by network/enterprise policy, so coverage here is arXiv-only, supplemented by web search.)

(3) GitHub eval-harness logs: Searched EleutherAI/lm-evaluation-harness and groq/openbench (a 95+-benchmark harness explicitly covering MMLU, GPQA, HumanEval, and competition math incl. AIME) for any committed run artifacts against GPT-5.5. Found no results.json, leaderboard entry, or PR referencing GPT-5.5 in either repo.

(4) Other leaderboards/aggregators: OpenRouter's gpt-5.5 and gpt-5.5-pro pages exist but render benchmark data client-side (not present in fetched content) and OpenRouter is known to source its numbers from Artificial Analysis, already exhausted in round 1. Scale AI SEAL: web search confirms GPT-5.5 has "no standardized public-set entry" on SEAL as of late June 2026 -- only a vendor-reported aggregate figure circulates (the same unsourced 88.7%/92.4% SWE-bench/MMLU pair already investigated and rejected in the prior round). LiveBench.ai and PapersWithCode returned no retrievable GPT-5.5 data (JS-rendered / redirect). AskClash.ai's dedicated 'gpt-5-5-xhigh' leaderboard page is JS-rendered and returned no populated benchmark values on fetch. LM Council (lmcouncil.ai/benchmarks) was the most substantive new find: it has explicit per-model tables for GPQA Diamond, SWE-bench Verified, FrontierMath Tiers 1-3/Tier 4, WeirdML v2, GSO, and OTIS Mock AIME 2024-25 for 'GPT-5.5 (xhigh)', but confirmed NO table for MMLU, MMLU-Pro, HumanEval, or HumanEval+ anywhere on the page, and its one MATH-adjacent table ('MATH Level 5') does not include GPT-5.5 (xhigh) at all. Its one AIME-adjacent entry, 'OTIS Mock AIME 2024-25' (GPT-5.5 (xhigh): 100.0% +/-0.0), was investigated and REJECTED for two independent reasons: (a) OTIS Mock AIME is a distinct benchmark from the taxonomy's 'aime' slug (\"American Invitational Mathematics Examination, pass@1\" -- the real competition), not an accepted proxy, and no otis/mock-aime slug exists in corpus/benchmarks.json to place it under instead; (b) the same page's FrontierMath figures for this exact model (Tiers 1-3: 85.3%, Tier 4: 72.5%) sharply contradict the well-corroborated benchlm.ai/Epoch AI figures already in this entry (51.7%/35.4%), a red flag strong enough to treat the entire source as unreliable/possibly synthetic rather than cite any of its numbers.

(5) Non-English-language sources: Chinese (Zhihu, chatgpt-china.org) and Japanese (oishillc.jp AI Lab OISHI complete guide, aipicks.jp) coverage was checked. None reports MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval numbers for GPT-5.5. Notably, oishillc.jp's independent Japanese-language review explicitly states (translated): \"The official System Card for GPT-5.5 includes no listings of Japanese benchmarks and major English benchmarks like MMLU lack specific reporting\" -- an independent non-English corroboration of the same absence the first pass already found on the English launch materials. Korean sources (gpters.org, wikidocs.net) likewise surfaced no MMLU/AIME-specific figures for GPT-5.5.

(6) Sibling-model comparison tables: layerlens.ai's GLM-5 benchmark review (a sibling-family comparison piece) does not include any GPT-5.5 comparison data at all. OpenRouter's GPT-5.5 Pro page (closest sibling variant) has no populated comparison table in its fetched content. No sibling-model source was found carrying a citable MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval number for the base or xhigh GPT-5.5 config.

Also encountered but explicitly rejected as non-citable during this pass (content-mill/SEO sites with no evaluation methodology, no source citations, or numbers already flagged unreliable in the prior round): automatio.ai (MMLU 92.5%, AIME 100%, MATH 98%, HumanEval 94.2% -- \"page lists no source citations... does not specify which reasoning effort level\"); o-mega.ai (MMLU 92.4%, the same unsourced figure already rejected in the 2026-07-16 pass); promptchief.tech (table structure present but data not retrievable, states scores are \"from official model cards and papers\" yet none of those actually report this). None of these meet the bar of a real, citable, independent methodology.

Conclusion: mmlu, math, aime, and humaneval remain omitted_no_evidence after this second, broader pass across all 6 prescribed new source types (HELM, arXiv/Semantic Scholar, GitHub eval-harness, other leaderboards/aggregators, non-English sources, sibling-model tables). This is consistent with the taxonomy's own framing of these four as saturated/retired frontier benchmarks that OpenAI and independent evaluators alike have stopped running or reporting for the GPT-5.5 generation.
